name: mcp-server
description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
capabilities: Guides building MCP servers in Python (FastMCP) and TypeScript (MCP SDK)
Provides complete implementation examples with code patterns
Includes evaluation framework for testing MCP server effectiveness
Offers security and authentication best practices
Supports multiple transport types: stdio, HTTP, SSE
author: Cam10001110101
securitySummary: This is a documentation skill for MCP server development. All 593 static findings are FALSE POSITIVEs: markdown code blocks triggered 'backtick execution' alerts; official MCP documentation URLs triggered 'hardcoded URL' alerts; UUIDs and version strings in docs triggered 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts. The Python scripts (connections.py, evaluation.py) use official MCP libraries for legitimate stdio transport configuration. No malicious behavior confirmed after manual review. Previous audit also rated this as 'safe'.
valueStatement: Create secure, efficient MCP servers that enable AI agents to interact with external APIs and services. Follow proven patterns for tool design, error handling, and evaluation.
userTitle: Build MCP Servers with Best Practices
limitations: Requires programming knowledge to implement servers
External API integration needs proper authentication setup

useCases.title: Integrate External Services
useCases.description: Build MCP servers to connect Claude with your APIs, databases, or third-party services.

useCases.title: Create Custom AI Tools
useCases.description: Develop specialized tools that enable AI agents to perform complex workflows and access real-time data.

useCases.title: Secure AI Integration
useCases.description: Implement enterprise-grade MCP servers with proper authentication, error handling, and monitoring.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Server
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a basic MCP server in Python that can search for users. Include proper tool naming, Pydantic validation, and error handling.

promptTemplates.title: API Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an MCP server for GitHub API integration. Show tools for searching repos, creating issues, and managing PRs with rate limiting.

promptTemplates.title: Evaluation Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an evaluation test suite with 10 realistic test cases to verify Claude can use my MCP server tools effectively.

promptTemplates.title: Production Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through deploying an MCP server in production with OAuth authentication, input validation, and monitoring.

bestPractices: Use service-specific prefixes in tool names to avoid conflicts
Implement both JSON and Markdown response formats for flexibility
Add proper annotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint) to all tools
Follow the evaluation-driven development approach for continuous improvement
antiPatterns: Don't create tools that simply wrap single API endpoints without workflow consideration
Avoid returning excessive data that overwhelms the context window
Never skip input validation or error handling
Don't ignore the CHARACTER_LIMIT when returning large datasets

faq.question: What programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The guide covers Python (FastMCP) and Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK) implementations.

faq.question: How do I test my MCP server?
faq.answer: Use the included evaluation framework with XML test cases to verify tool effectiveness.

faq.question: What transport types are available?
faq.answer: MCP supports stdio, HTTP, and SSE transports for different deployment scenarios.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication?
faq.answer: The guide covers OAuth, API keys, and secure credential management for service integration.

faq.question: What is the difference between tools and resources?
faq.answer: Tools perform actions and operations while resources provide static data access via URI templates.

faq.question: How do I prevent tool name conflicts?
faq.answer: Always use service-specific prefixes like github_create_issue instead of generic names.
